2605.07903.title: BeeVe: Unsupervised Acoustic State Discovery in Honey Bee Buzzing
2605.07903.paper_summary: Discovering structure in biological signals without supervision is a fundamental problem in computational intelligence, yet existing bioacoustic methods assume vocal production models or predefined semantic units, leaving non-vocal species poorly served. This work introduces BeeVe, an unsupervised framework for acoustic state discovery in collective honey bee buzzing. BeeVe uses the self-supervised Patchout Spectrogram Transformer (PaSST) as a frozen feature extractor, then trains a Vector-Quantized Variational Autoencoder (VQ-VAE) without labels on those embeddings, learning a finite discrete codebook of acoustic tokens directly from unlabelled hive audio. No labels, pretext tasks, or contrastive objectives are used at any stage. Post-hoc evaluation against known queen status reveals that the learned tokens separate queenright and queenless conditions with Jensen-Shannon Divergence values between 0.609 and 0.688, and that the queenless condition further decomposes into three internally coherent sub-states stable across experiments with different codebook sizes and random seeds. Token transition analysis confirms non-random sequential structure (p << 0.001) across all experiments. Generalisation to unseen recordings preserves both token overlap (Jaccard = 0.947) and global manifold topology. These results demonstrate that unsupervised discrete codebook learning can recover repeatable acoustic structure from a non-vocal biological signal without annotation, opening a path toward non-invasive acoustic hive health monitoring.
2605.07903.paper_summary_zh: 發現生物信號中的結構而不需監督是一個計算智能中的基本問題，然而現有的生物聲學方法假設了聲音產生模型或預定義的語義單元，導致非聲音物種的服務不足。這項工作介紹了BeeVe，一個用於集體蜜蜂嗡嗡聲的無監督聲音狀態發現框架。BeeVe使用自我監督的Patchout Spectrogram Transformer (PaSST)作為凍結的特徵提取器，然後在這些嵌入上無標籤地訓練向量量化變分自編碼器（VQ-VAE），直接從未標記的蜂巢音頻中學習有限的離散聲音代碼本。任何階段都不使用標籤、前提任務或對比目標。針對已知的女王狀態進行的事後評估顯示，學習到的代碼在女王在和女王缺失條件之間以Jensen-Shannon Divergence值在0.609和0.688之間分離，且女王缺失的條件進一步分解為三個在不同代碼本大小和隨機種子實驗中穩定的內部一致子狀態。代碼轉換分析確認了所有實驗中非隨機的序列結構（p << 0.001）。對未見錄音的泛化保留了代碼重疊（Jaccard = 0.947）和全局流形拓撲。這些結果證明無監督的離散代碼本學習可以從非聲音生物信號中恢復可重複的聲音結構而無需註釋，為非侵入式的蜂巢健康監測開闢了一條道路。
2605.07903.author: Hamze Hammami et.al.
